markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Close EXT-MC-001 (prose multi-cursor/multi-selection editing - implement via ProseMirror multi-selection plugin approach; if true multi-caret is infeasible in ProseMirror, deliver multi-range selection + simultaneous edit application and DOCUMENT the deviation honestly), ED-CODE-INT-008 (Monaco multi-cursor/column-select verified enabled), EXT-SNIP-001 (snippet system: define/insert/tab-stops for prose+code), ED-WB-008 (zen/focus mode), plus table editing completion for ED-CORE-002 (add/remove row/col, header toggle, cell merge).
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.
Bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries only (Tiptap/Monaco precedent); no CDN; no external app launches as product behavior.
PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; UI artifacts are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle (v02.193), product code, tests, and receipts.

OPERATOR ORDER (DEC-007, 2026-06-12): NO scaffolding, templates, mock-ups, or vibecoding; every feature must be working AND tested in runtime (executed vitest/Playwright/real-PG proof per feature, not status text). A feature without an executed runtime proof is NOT done.
No fake multi-cursor visuals: actual simultaneous edit application or an honestly documented deviation in the MT evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: Power editing parity: multiple selections edited at once, snippets expand with tab-stops, zen mode, full table manipulation.
acceptance_criteria: Table ops proven on real docs incl. undo.
Adversarial self-review recorded per the WP loop; findings fixed before evidence write.
verification: pnpm vitest run green; tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.
Offline Playwright runtime proof for each named capability (zero external network requests).
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests run with exact names/counts, commits.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence and Playwright artifacts produced.
List remaining risks and any honestly-documented deviations (e.g., prose multi-cursor approach).

evidence.summary: Claimed MT-251 for a first direct product slice: ED-WB-008 zen/focus mode in the rich editor, using existing MT-244 editor surfaces and offline frontend runtime proof. MT-251 remains IN_PROGRESS; multi-cursor, snippets, Monaco column select, and full table-editing completion are not yet claimed as complete.

Implemented and hardened the ED-WB-008 focus-mode slice of MT-251: RichTextEditor now has a toolbar focus toggle, hides ordinary toolbar controls while leaving an exit control, closes overflow/palette/export/find surfaces when entering focus mode, clears pending argument prompts, suppresses shortcut-opened and external-request transient panels while focused, and applies responsive focus-surface styling with browser screenshot proof. MT-251 remains IN_PROGRESS; prose multi-selection, snippets/tab-stops, Monaco column-select proof, and full table operation completion remain open.

diff_attack_surfaces: Command catalog producer to overflow-menu consumer parity for tableEdit commands.
Tiptap canRun/run parity outside tables, inside single-cell selections, and inside multi-cell selections.
History grouping and exact undo restoration for table structure/header/merge/split mutations.
Built harness runtime proof using real rendered editor controls rather than catalog-only unit tests.
findings: Initial adversarial review failed the slice because undo coverage was incomplete, invalid-state coverage was missing for new table commands, and the proof depended on table mutation counts only.
Findings were fixed before this evidence write by adding exact editor.getJSON undo restoration, invalid canRun coverage, and a Playwright real-harness table operation proof.

diff_attack_surfaces: Shared Monaco setup defaults affect every createConfiguredEditor consumer, including embedded code blocks and standalone harnesses.
Column-selection proof can be falsely satisfied by option presence alone unless runtime typing proves simultaneous same-column edits.
Windows CRLF keyboard input can make text assertions fail for platform reasons unrelated to Monaco behavior.
Caller override order can accidentally disable the intended defaults if options are merged incorrectly.
findings: No remaining blocker found for the Monaco slice after validation.
Initial Playwright failure was a platform line-ending assertion defect; fixed by normalizing line endings while preserving strict content expectations.

The createConfiguredEditor option defaults are set before caller overrides, so specialized future callers can still intentionally override them; current embedded code-block callers inherit the MT-251 behavior.

diff_attack_surfaces: Snippet-template parsing to ProseMirror absolute text ranges can drift if inserted text start is computed from the pre-insert selection rather than the post-insert document.
Prose placeholder edits can invalidate later tab stops unless transaction mapping remaps active ranges after document changes.
RichTextEditor Tab interception can regress editor ergonomics if it fires without an active prose snippet session or inside embedded Monaco.
Code snippets can falsely look implemented if inserted as plain text instead of using Monaco SnippetController2, because Tab traversal would then leave Monaco snippet semantics unproven.
Command catalog and overflow UI producer/consumer wiring can omit the new snippet category even when the helper functions pass unit tests.
findings: No remaining blocker found for the EXT-SNIP-001 slice after validation.
An earlier off-by-one prose selection defect selected " Topi" instead of "Topic"; fixed by deriving inserted text start from the post-insert document.

The first full rich_editor_typing rerun exposed a non-reproduced H1 boot wait failure outside the snippet tests; the exact H1 rerun passed and the full suite rerun passed, so this is recorded as residual harness timing risk rather than a snippet blocker.
The code snippet browser assertion expects Monaco's real adjusted whitespace output (four spaces) rather than a literal tab, matching the runtime document state.
independent_checks_run: Built-asset probe after fresh pnpm run build:harness found snippet.prose.meeting, snippet.code.function, data-snippet-active, and monaco-code-snippet- in the emitted RichTextEditor asset.

rg over app/src and tests/dependency_policy/rich_editor_typing.spec.ts confirmed producer/consumer wiring for insertProseSnippet, moveToNextProseSnippetTabStop, SnippetController2, data-snippet-active, snippet.prose.meeting, and snippet.code.function.
The invalid-snippet negative-path unit case proves code.function and missing.snippet are declined by the prose insertion path without mutating the document.
counterfactual_checks: If app/src/lib/editor/editor_snippets.ts stopped remapping active tab-stop ranges through transaction.mapping, the prose snippet unit and browser tests would fail after replacing Topic with Roadmap before moving to Owner.

If app/src/components/RichTextEditor.tsx stopped including the snippet category in overflowCommands or stopped handling active prose snippet Tab, the browser prose snippet test would fail before selecting Owner.

If app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx replaced SnippetController2.get(instance)?.insert with plain text insertion, the code snippet browser test would fail because Tab would not move through name, args, and body placeholders.
If app/src/vite-env.d.ts removed the Monaco snippet-controller declaration, tsc --noEmit would fail on the internal SnippetController2 import.
boundary_probes: Definition-to-command boundary checked: EDITOR_SNIPPETS backs editor_commands snippet entries and filterEditorCommands("snippet") exposes the prose command.
Command-to-rendered-UI boundary checked: RichTextEditor overflow includes category snippet and the browser test clicks overflow-cmd-snippet.prose.meeting on the built harness.
Monaco bridge boundary checked: MonacoCodeBlockView unit test mocks SnippetController2 and asserts the exact native snippet template passed to insert.
Build/runtime boundary checked: rebuilt dist-harness asset contains the snippet identifiers and Playwright exercises the offline runtime with external network blocked.
negative_path_checks: insertProseSnippet(editor, "code.function") returns false and leaves the document unchanged.
insertProseSnippet(editor, "missing.snippet") returns false and leaves the document unchanged.
moveToNextProseSnippetTabStop returns false when no prose snippet session is active.
monacoSnippetTemplateForId("prose.meeting") returns null so prose snippets cannot be routed through the Monaco snippet path.
adversarial_review.residual_uncertainty: This review does not cover EXT-MC-001 prose multi-selection/simultaneous edit application. The code snippet implementation imports Monaco's internal SnippetController2 path; local unit, type, build, and browser proof pin current behavior, but Monaco upgrades could require revalidation. The first full rich_editor_typing rerun had one non-reproduced pre-existing H1 boot wait failure before the exact and full reruns passed.

blocked: MT-251 remains IN_PROGRESS; this evidence validates EXT-SNIP-001 plus the earlier focus-mode, table-editing, and Monaco multi-cursor/column-selection slices.
Remaining MT-251 work: EXT-MC-001 prose multi-selection/simultaneous edit application or an honestly documented deviation.

evidence.summary: Closed the remaining EXT-MC-001 scope and advanced MT-251 to READY_FOR_VALIDATION, not PASS. The implementation does not fake native discontiguous ProseMirror carets; it delivers the contract's allowed fallback: operator-stored prose multi-range selections rendered as decorations, plus one simultaneous transaction that applies typed text to every stored range and keeps the range state observable through the editor debug attributes. With this final slice, MT-251 has runtime proof for focus mode, table editing, Monaco column selection, prose/code snippets with tab-stops, and prose multi-range simultaneous edit application.

diff_attack_surfaces: ProseMirror supports one native selection; claiming true multi-caret behavior without custom state/decorations would be false parity.
Stored ranges can become stale or overlap after edits unless normalized, rejected, or mapped through transaction mappings.
Simultaneous edits can corrupt positions unless replacements are applied in descending document order and collapsed follow-up positions are computed from original ordered ranges.
Command producer/consumer wiring can pass helper tests while failing operator reachability if the selection category is not exposed through the overflow surface.
The text-input handler can steal ordinary typing if it does not short-circuit when no multi-range state exists.
findings: No blocker remains for MT-251 after the EXT-MC-001 slice.
The first implementation attempt over-blocked text nodes because ProseMirror text nodes are leaf/atom-like; fixed by excluding node.isText from the atom guard.
The first typecheck used brittle internal Plugin spec type extraction; fixed with the explicit Mapping type from @tiptap/pm/transform.
The delivered behavior is intentionally documented as multi-range selection plus simultaneous edit application, not fake native discontiguous caret rendering.
independent_checks_run: Built-asset probe after fresh harness build found selection.addRange, selection.clearRanges, data-multi-range-count, hs-multi-range-selection, and hsProseMultiRangeSelection in emitted assets.
Full offline browser typing spec proved the new prose multi-range flow alongside the earlier MT-251 Monaco column-selection and snippet flows: 11 passed.
Visual regression suite passed after adding the multi-range decoration style: 6 passed.
counterfactual_checks: If app/src/lib/tiptap/prose_multi_range_selection.ts removed handleTextInput, the browser multi-range test would store two ranges but typing X would not replace both Intro and typed.
If replacements were applied in ascending order instead of descending order, later stored ranges would be edited at stale positions after earlier replacements changed document length.
If app/src/components/RichTextEditor.tsx omitted selection from overflowCommands, the browser test would fail at overflow-cmd-selection.addRange even though the helper unit tests passed.
If build_editor_extensions.ts omitted ProseMultiRangeSelection, the command state and data-multi-range-count surface would remain inert in the integrated editor.
boundary_probes: Helper-to-plugin boundary checked by unit tests: addProseMultiRange stores noncontiguous ranges, applyProseMultiRangeText edits both in one undoable transaction, and invalid/overlapping ranges are declined.
Command-to-UI boundary checked by browser proof: selected prose ranges are added through the rendered overflow command surface.
Runtime-to-artifact boundary checked by dist-harness asset probe and offline Playwright, with external network blocked by the dependency-policy config.
negative_path_checks: applyProseMultiRangeText returns false when no stored ranges exist.
Collapsed ranges are rejected when added by the operator path.
Overlapping ranges are rejected without mutating the document.
Ranges that include atom/code-block nodes are rejected by the prose-only guard.
adversarial_review.residual_uncertainty: The fallback intentionally does not render true native multi-carets; collapsed follow-up ranges remain observable by data-multi-range-count but are not visible as caret widgets. Backspace/delete/paste across stored prose ranges are not claimed in MT-251; the proven simultaneous edit path is typed text input plus direct helper application.

deviations: True ProseMirror discontiguous caret rendering was not implemented. The accepted fallback is multi-range selection plus simultaneous edit application, with decorations for non-collapsed ranges and machine-readable range count state.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger and/or offline Playwright where the contract names durable state or GUI behavior.
